subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Player-Count Audit
- **Supported counts** — 1 to 5 players.
- **Scaling** — The companion app adjusts monster spawns and difficulty.
- **Solo mode** — Fully supported; control 1 investigator.

#### Required App
- **Mansions of Madness companion app** — Required for all play; manages map, monsters, puzzles, and story.
- **Platforms** — Available on iOS, Android, Mac, and Steam.

#### Investigators
- **Investigator figures** — 8 plastic investigator miniatures.
- **Investigator cards** — Each investigator has unique stats: Strength, Agility, Observation, Lore, Influence.
- **Health and Sanity** — Each investigator has separate Health and Sanity values.

#### Map Components
- **Map tiles** — Large double-sided modular map tiles representing rooms and corridors.
- **Exploration tokens** — Mark unexplored points of interest.
- **Search tokens** — Mark searchable locations.
- **Sight tokens** — Track line of sight and area connections.
- **Door tokens** — Represent locked, barricaded, or secret doors.

#### Monster Components
- **Monster figures** — Plastic miniatures for various Lovecraftian horrors.
- **Monster tokens** — Tokens representing monster positions on the map.

#### Cards and Tokens
- **Common item cards** — Weapons, tools, and supplies.
- **Unique item cards** — Powerful one-of-a-kind items.
- **Spell cards** — Magical abilities with risk/reward mechanics.
- **Condition cards** — Status effects (wounded, stunned, restrained, etc.).
- **Insanity cards** — Secret insanity conditions that change behavior.
- **Damage/horror tokens** — Track physical and mental damage.
- **Clue tokens** — Story-critical discoveries.
- **Dice** — Custom 8-sided dice for skill tests.

subtopics.title: Board Layout
subtopics.content:
#### Scenario Setup
1. **Launch app** — Open the companion app and select a scenario.
2. **Choose investigators** — Select investigators in the app.
3. **App-guided setup** — The app displays the starting map configuration.
4. **Place map tiles** — Assemble the starting map tiles as shown.
5. **Place tokens** — Add exploration tokens, search tokens, and door tokens per app instructions.
6. **Starting positions** — Place investigator figures on the starting area.
7. **Initial items** — Distribute starting items as indicated by the app.

#### Progressive Map Reveal
- **Exploration** — New rooms are revealed when investigators explore marked locations.
- **App manages layout** — The app shows which tiles to place and how to orient them.
- **Story events** — Room reveals often trigger narrative events and monster spawns.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
#### Investigator Selection
- **Choose investigator** — Each player selects 1 investigator.
- **Stat card** — Take your investigator's card showing Strength, Agility, Observation, Lore, and Influence.
- **Health pool** — Note your maximum Health (physical resilience).
- **Sanity pool** — Note your maximum Sanity (mental resilience).
- **Starting items** — Receive any starting items assigned by the app.

--- 1 Player ---
- **Solo** — Control 1 investigator; the app adjusts difficulty.

--- 2-5 Players ---
- **One each** — Each player controls 1 investigator.
- **Recommendation** — 3-4 investigators provides the best balance of exploration coverage and combat ability.

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content:
#### Scenario Start
- **Narrative introduction** — The app presents the scenario's story setup.
- **Objective hint** — Initial clues about what investigators must discover or accomplish.
- **Multiple scenarios** — The base game includes several scenarios; expansions add more.
- **Begin play** — The app indicates when to begin the first investigation phase.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
#### Round Structure
1. **Investigation Phase** — Each investigator takes a turn (in any order chosen by players).
2. **Mythos Phase** — Press the app button to trigger the Mythos Phase; the app controls enemy actions and story events.

#### Investigator Turn
- **2 actions** — Each investigator may take up to 2 actions per turn.
- **Action types** — Move, explore, search, interact, attack, use component, trade, push.
- **No duplicate actions** — You may not perform the same action twice (exception: move can be taken twice).

#### Mythos Phase (App-Controlled)
- **Monster activation** — The app moves and attacks with each monster.
- **Mythos events** — Environmental effects, story triggers, and timer advancements.
- **Horror effects** — Certain events force Horror checks on investigators.

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
#### Movement
- **Move action** — Move up to 2 spaces.
- **Spaces** — Each room tile section counts as 1 space.
- **Doors** — Some doors require interaction to open.
- **Double move** — Move is the only action you can take twice per turn.

#### Exploration and Search
- **Explore** — Interact with an exploration token; the app reveals what you find (new rooms, clues, events).
- **Search** — Interact with a search token; the app provides items or information.

#### Combat
- **Attack action** — Attack a monster in your space.
- **Weapon required** — Use a weapon card to determine your dice pool and damage type.
- **Skill test** — Roll dice equal to the relevant stat (Strength for melee, Agility for ranged, etc.).
- **Success** — Each die showing a 5+ (on d8) counts as 1 success; compare to monster's required hits.
- **App resolution** — Input successes into the app; it resolves damage and effects.

#### Skill Tests
- **Test types** — Strength, Agility, Observation, Lore, or Influence.
- **Dice pool** — Roll dice equal to your stat value.
- **Successes** — Count results of 5 or higher (on 8-sided dice).
- **App evaluation** — The app tells you the outcome based on your successes.

#### Interaction
- **Component interaction** — Interact with map objects, NPCs, or story elements as indicated by the app.
- **Puzzle solving** — Some interactions trigger in-app puzzles (sliding puzzles, lock combinations, code breaking).

#### Trade
- **Trade action** — Exchange items with another investigator in the same space.

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
#### Health and Sanity (Dual Track)
- **Physical damage** — Facedown damage tokens reduce Health.
- **Mental horror** — Facedown horror tokens reduce Sanity.
- **Face-up wounds** — Some damage and horror tokens are flipped face up, applying specific conditions.
- **Health zero** — An investigator reduced to 0 Health is wounded, then eliminated if wounded again.
- **Sanity zero** — An investigator reduced to 0 Sanity becomes insane.

#### Insanity System
- **Insanity card** — When Sanity reaches 0, draw an Insanity card.
- **Secret condition** — Insanity cards give the player a secret new behavior or win condition.
- **Changed goals** — Some insanity conditions make the investigator work against the team.
- **Still playing** — Insane investigators remain in the game but may act unpredictably.

#### Horror Checks
- **Monster encounter** — When a monster appears or attacks, investigators may need to make Horror checks.
- **Lore test** — Roll Lore stat dice; failures result in horror tokens.
- **Sanity drain** — Failed Horror checks bring investigators closer to insanity.

#### Spell Casting
- **Spell cards** — Investigators with spells can cast them for powerful effects.
- **Risk element** — Spells often require a Lore check; failure can have negative consequences.
- **Spell variety** — Attack spells, protection spells, and utility spells available.

#### App-Managed Elements
- **Monster AI** — The app controls all monster movement and attack patterns.
- **Story branching** — Investigator choices during events affect the scenario narrative.
- **Timer/doom** — The app tracks hidden timers that advance the threat level.

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### Scenario Victory
- **Complete objective** — The app announces victory when investigators solve the mystery or complete the scenario goal.
- **Variable objectives** — Different scenarios require investigation, combat, escape, or ritual completion.

#### Scenario Defeat
- **All eliminated** — All investigators are eliminated (Health and Sanity depleted).
- **Timer expired** — The app's doom/threat timer reaches its endpoint.
- **Story failure** — Certain narrative choices can lead to scenario failure.

#### No Campaign Scoring
- **Standalone scenarios** — Each scenario is a self-contained story.
- **Win/lose binary** — Scenarios end in success or failure with no point scoring.
- **Replayability** — Scenarios have randomized elements that change on replay.
- **Expansion scenarios** — Additional scenarios available through expansion content.

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
- **Spread out to explore** — Cover the map quickly to find clues before the Mythos phase escalates.
- **Prioritize exploration tokens** — These advance the story and reveal new areas.
- **Assign roles by stats** — Send high-Observation investigators to search; high-Strength to fight; high-Lore to cast spells.
- **Manage horror early** — Horror accumulates faster than physical damage; protect Sanity.
- **Collect items** — Search tokens provide weapons and tools critical for later encounters.

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
- **Ignoring Sanity** — Insanity can derail the team more than physical wounds.
- **Grouping too tightly** — Staying together wastes time; the app timer advances regardless.
- **Fighting every monster** — Some monsters are better avoided or evaded while pursuing the objective.
- **Hoarding items** — Share items with investigators who have the best stats to use them.
- **Neglecting puzzle preparation** — Some puzzles require specific items or clues gathered earlier; explore thoroughly.

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content:
#### Decision 1: Investigate vs Fight
- **Investigate** — Focus on clues and story progression to solve the mystery quickly.
- **Fight** — Clear monsters to create safe paths and reduce ongoing Mythos threats.

#### Decision 2: Split Up vs Stay Together
- **Split up** — Cover more ground and explore faster.
- **Stay together** — Safety in numbers against powerful monsters.

#### Decision 3: Health vs Sanity Preservation
- **Protect Health** — Avoid physical confrontation to stay alive.
- **Protect Sanity** — Avoid Horror checks and Mythos exposure to prevent insanity.
